01 — About

Who I Am

I'm a pre-final year Computer Science student at Nehru Institute of Technology, Coimbatore, with hands-on experience from a React JS internship and a string of independent Python builds. I hold Oracle certifications in Cloud and AI foundations, Git and Linux training from IIT Bombay's Spoken Tutorial Project, and four paper-presentation awards.

My interest sits at the intersection of Python debugging and project-driven development — I like taking something broken (or nonexistent) and building it into something that works end to end.

LocationCoimbatore, Tamil Nadu, India
EducationB.E. CSE — CGPA 7.6
LanguagesEnglish · Tamil · Malayalam

03 — Experience

Where I've Worked

Mar 2026 – Apr 2026

React JS Intern

Virtual Tech Services, Chennai (Corporate Office)

  • Completed a structured, on-site internship at the company's corporate office, progressing from core React fundamentals — JSX, props, and component lifecycle — to shipping a complete, production-style application.
  • Designed and built an "Online Ticket Booking System" as the capstone project: reusable, composable components, client-side state management, form validation, and an end-to-end seat-selection-to-confirmation booking flow.
  • Translated functional requirements straight from mentors into working screens, running daily check-ins and iterating quickly on UI and logic feedback rather than waiting for a single big review.
  • Practiced debugging component and state-related issues under real deadlines, tracing re-render bugs and prop-drilling problems back to their root cause instead of patching symptoms.
  • Picked up the day-to-day habits of a front-end team: component-first thinking, keeping UI state predictable, and writing code that a mentor could review and hand back with confidence.

04 — Projects

Things I've Built

🏨

Hotel Management System

A Python desktop application that runs the core day-to-day operations of a small hotel from a single interface.

  • Tracks room availability in real time and updates status the moment a guest checks in or out.
  • Gives every guest and every room its own persistent record, so booking history and current occupancy are one lookup away.
  • Built with a clear object-oriented structure to keep guest, room, and booking logic cleanly separated.
PythonOOPDesktop App
🚌

Bus Ticket Booking System

A console-based simulation of a real-world bus reservation counter, from route search to confirmed seat.

  • Lets a user browse available buses, pick a seat, and review full booking details before confirming.
  • Uses conditional logic and variable-based seat tracking to stop two passengers from ever booking the same seat.
  • Designed the booking flow to mirror how a real ticketing counter validates a request end to end.
PythonLogicCLI
➗

Arithmetic Operations Utility

A general-purpose calculation tool built around one reusable interface instead of one function per operation.

  • Handles a range of everyday and mathematical operations through a single consistent entry point.
  • Accounts for numeric edge cases — division by zero, invalid input, type mismatches — so it fails gracefully instead of crashing.
  • Structured to make adding a new operation type a small, isolated change rather than a rewrite.
PythonUtility
🎫

Online Ticket Booking System

My React JS internship capstone — a production-style booking app taken from wireframe to working flow.

  • Built with reusable, composable React components and centralized client-side state management.
  • Implements an end-to-end booking journey: browse, select, validate, and confirm, with feedback at every step.
  • Refined through mentor feedback cycles, with a focus on debugging state and prop-related issues under deadline.
React JSFrontendState Mgmt
